openmeter/notification/eventhandler/noop.purpose: No-op implementation of notification.EventHandler used when the notification subsystem is disabled or unconfigured. All methods return nil, satisfying the interface contract without performing any real dispatch, reconciliation, or lifecycle work.

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: The file-level var _ notification.EventHandler = (*Handler)(nil) ensures compile-time verification that Handler fully implements the interface.

patterns.name: No-op method bodies
patterns.description: Every method accepts all parameters (using blank identifiers) and returns nil — never logs, never panics, never mutates state.
patterns.example: func (h Handler) Dispatch(_ context.Context, _ *notification.Event) error { return nil }

patterns.name: Constructor returns (*Handler, error)
patterns.description: New() follows the standard two-value constructor signature matching the Wire provider expectation, even though no error can occur.

key_file_guides.role: Single-file package that provides the noop EventHandler; wired by app/common when notification is disabled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: If notification.EventHandler gains new methods, this file will fail to compile — add matching no-op stubs immediately.

anti_patterns: Adding logging, metrics, or side-effects to any method — this is intentionally inert.
Removing the compile-time interface assertion (var _ ...) — it is the only guard against interface drift.
Using context.Background() instead of the received context parameter — blank-identify it instead.
Storing any state on Handler — it must remain a zero-value struct.

decisions.decision: Value receiver on Handler, not pointer receiver for most methods
decisions.rationale: Handler holds no state; value receivers avoid unnecessary heap allocation and communicate that the struct is safe to copy.

decisions.decision: Separate noop sub-package rather than a nil check inside the real handler
decisions.rationale: Matches the broader noop pattern used across openmeter (ledgernoop, webhooknoop) — Wire wires the concrete noop type so call sites need no nil guards at runtime.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new method required by notification.EventHandler after an interface change
